name: Mort's Quiche
recipeInstructions:
1. Clarify a quarter pound of butter and divide it into two portions: 3/4 for mushrooms and 1/4 for onions.
2. Add salt, pepper, Worcestershire sauce, soy sauce, teriyaki sauce, cinnamon (about half a teaspoon), and milk to the respective pans with butter.
3. Cook the mushrooms until very brown and caramelized; they will reconstitute after baking.
4. Brown the onions on high heat. Reduce heat, cover with beer for about 5 minutes, uncover, and reduce further.
5. Combine mushrooms and onions thoroughly.
6. Prepare pastry: mix flour, butter, egg yolks, brown sugar, and milk (or use less). Form into a ball, knead gently, and roll out to fit the ring pan.
7. Place the dough in the pan, trim edges, brush with egg white, and prick all over with a fork.
8. Combine sour cream or whipping cream, egg yolks, grated cheddar cheese, nutmeg, and cinnamon in a bowl.
9. Arrange cheddar cheese in the bottom of the pastry shell, add mushroom/onion mixture, pour custard, sprinkle more cheese, and garnish with green onions.
10. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C) and bake for 40-50 minutes, rotating occasionally, until browned and set.
Note: The recipe does not specify a ring pan size in the original text, so it is assumed to be based on common quiche sizes.
